bag of decisions is made by considering expected values of different strategies  
bag of risk is involved in both strategy a and strategy b presented to the author  
bag of strategy a has a 0.4 chance of winning 1000 and a 0.6 chance of losing 500  
bag of strategy b has a 0.6 chance of winning 800 and a 0.4 chance of losing 200  
bag of expected value for strategy a is calculated by multiplying probabilities by outcomes  
bag of expected value for strategy a is 0.4 times 1000 minus 0.6 times 500  
bag of expected value for strategy a is 400 minus 300 which equals 100  
bag of expected value for strategy b is calculated similarly with its probabilities and outcomes  
bag of expected value for strategy b is 0.6 times 800 minus 0.4 times 200  
bag of expected value for strategy b is 480 minus 80 which equals 400
